title: coreopsis douglasii
intro: Coreopsis douglasii, also known as the Douglas' tickseed, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Coreopsis douglasii are said to include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Coreopsis douglasii include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
